goal: First-pass live repair after NAS OpenClaw audit: restore useful journal retention, add gateway resource guards, and fix a systemd unit ownership anomaly without broad architecture changes.

liveChanges.change: Added journald drop-in with SystemMaxUse=200M, SystemMaxFileSize=50M, MaxRetentionSec=14day.
Changed owner from UNKNOWN:staff to root:root; mode remains 0644.

postRepairFindings: adminAI journald visibility is restored after restart; startup and channel logs are now visible in journal.
Both gateway health and ready endpoints returned healthy after startup windows.

adminAI Telegram startup still logged deleteWebhook/setMyCommands network failures. Direct Telegram/OpenAI egress timed out, while the local HTTP proxy reached Telegram/OpenAI public endpoints, so follow-up should focus on app/proxy integration rather than raw proxy availability.
Both gateways log a security warning: tools.exec.applyPatch.workspaceOnly=false.
Both gateways log an update notice: latest v2026.4.27, current v2026.4.9.
Old openclaw-gateway.service cleanup, e2b keepwarm enablement, and Ollama relay architecture changes were intentionally deferred.
rollback: Restore files from /root/openclaw-repair-backups/20260430T161559+0800-codex-l3-first-pass, or remove the newly added drop-ins.
Run systemctl daemon-reload after restoring/removing systemd unit drop-ins.
Run systemctl restart systemd-journald.service if reverting journald retention.
Run systemctl restart openclaw-adminai-gateway.service openclaw-benben.service if reverting gateway resource guards.
Restore /etc/systemd/system/openclaw-memory-maintenance.service ownership to the backed-up owner only if a compatibility issue appears.
deferred: Investigate adminAI Telegram command sync network failures.
Plan OpenClaw version update from v2026.4.9 to v2026.4.27.
Clean or archive old openclaw-gateway.service residue after a separate dependency check.
Add a standard health endpoint for openclaw-ollama-relay.service.
